signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.356 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the structural bid, but Middle East de-escalation headlines drive a -0.96% intraday retreat as the USD safe-haven premium erodes. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (long%=30.6%) remains a crowded carry trade vulnerable to further unwinds if the WSJ-reported 14-point MOU between the US and Iran is finalized. Carry dominates the long-term regime while VIX remains at 17.4 (normal volatility), but intervention risk and geopolitical shifts cap upside near 160.00.
signals.watch level: Watch for a break of 155.00 to accelerate spec short covering ahead of the June 16 BoJ meeting.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 bilateral theme active as NZD outperforms (+0.537%) while CAD lags (-0.557%). The 1.094% spread is driven by a hawkish RBNZ bias (hike bias active for May 27) vs a neutral BoC at 2.25%. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) vs a hawkish CB bias creates a short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ signals a hike, while CAD remains pressured by the -5.96% intraday drop in WTI Oil to $96.2.
signals.watch level: Next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is the primary catalyst for NZD trend continuation.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7240 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 89% short represents a significant institutional vs retail divergence. Retail is aggressively fading the post-RBA hike extension to 2022 highs. With a -60bp carry disadvantage vs the Fed (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), the pair relies on risk-on sentiment and commodity support; retail capitulation risk rises if price holds above 0.7250.
signals.watch level: Watch for retail short reduction to signal a local top or 0.7250 break for extension.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.720 vs 252d norm of -0.145 (z=+1.96σ). The pair is decoupling from traditional macro drivers to trade as a pure risk-proxy alongside European equities. Despite the 1D +0.47% move to 1.1752, upside is structurally limited by the +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%).
signals.watch level: Watch for a reversion in equity correlation or the June 11 ECB meeting to reset the regime.
